title: 360-Degree Fire Protection — Pan-Tilt Nozzle with Flame Sensor Array
design_question: How to implement automatic 360-degree fire detection and directional suppression?
selected_approach: 4× YG1006 photodiode flame sensors at 90° intervals (360° coverage). Arduino Mega computes fire azimuth from relative sensor signals. Pan-tilt gimbal (2× MG996R servos, 0–360° pan, 0–90° tilt) aims suppression nozzle. 12 V solenoid valve opens water supply on confirmed flame detection (2+ sensors active or single sensor > 3× background). 2 s burst suppression, then recheck. Buzzer + LED alarm. Manual override button.
rationale: 4-sensor array provides 360° coverage with 90° resolution, sufficient to localise fire quadrant for nozzle aiming. MG996R servos achieve fast pan (0.17 s/60°) for rapid response. Confirmation logic prevents false activations from sunlight or hot surfaces.

alternatives.approach: Single pan-tilt sensor without fixed array

verification_notes: FACT: '360-Degree Fire Detection and Suppression System' gives θ_fire = arctan2(S_y, S_x) localisation method.
